source_jobs.title: Legal Intake Specialist (Bilingual - English/Spanish)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FINCH

We believe every American household deserves access to counsel in life’s biggest moments. At Finch, we’re building the infrastructure to make justice radically more accessible. Our modern approach to consumer law automates the admin work and puts clients first, starting with personal injury.

In just over a year, we've grown 10x, raised a $20M Series A, and become the pre-litigation partner of choice for top personal injury firms across the country. We believe the best outcomes happen when expert operators and purpose-built AI work together – which is why we handle every step of pre-lit, from intake and claim opening to medical records, lien management, and demands, with humans leading every case.

We’re backed by Sequoia, Redpoint, and the founders & CEOs of generational companies like DoorDash, Ironclad, and Digits. We’re rebuilding how the law serves everyday Americans from first principles, and we’re hiring exceptional operators to help us scale it nationwide.





THIS ROLE

Intake Specialists are the first voice potential clients hear from Finch. You’ll build trust in minutes, qualify cases, and ensure every eligible client signs quickly and seamlessly. This 24/7 team is the front line of Finch’s client experience.




YOU WILL:

 - Answer inbound calls with empathy and professionalism.

 - Qualify cases and confirm eligibility using defined criteria.

 - Guide clients through the sign-up process and secure retainers.

 - Hand off cases to attorneys and lead paralegals with complete and accurate information.

 - With your teammates, maintain responsiveness as a key builder of trust with every partner.




YOUR TEAM: LEGAL OPERATIONS

Legal Operations is responsible for delivering exceptional outcomes for our law firm partners and their injured clients with precision, speed & care. At Finch, we’re building a team of the top 1% of legal operators with experience from law firms across America, and enabling them with next-generation technology to serve more clients, with more humanity.




YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU:

 - Have 5+ years in client-facing intake or sales roles, ideally in legal services.

 - Are an excellent communicator who can build trust quickly.

 - Stay calm and focused under pressure.

 - Are comfortable working flexible schedules, including evenings and weekends.




COMPENSATION

The expected package for this role includes base salary + equity.

 - Base salary = $55,000 – $75,000.




BENEFITS

 - 100% coverage for health, dental, and vision.

 - 401(k) retirement plan.

 - Flexible PTO (we trust you to take the time you need).

 - (For those in NYC) In-office snacks, drinks, and daily team lunch and dinners.




INTERVIEW PROCESS

 1. Intro: An initial call to see if there's a mutual fit.

 2. Team Interview(s): 1-2 interviews with a team and/or operations lead.

 3. Final: An interview with one of our founders.




AT FINCH LEGAL, WE BELIEVE IN PRACTICING WHAT WE ADVOCATE.

As a company dedicated to upholding justice and protecting people in the workplace, we are equally committed to fostering a safe, inclusive, and equitable environment within our own walls. We welcome and support individuals from all backgrounds and lived experiences — regardless of race, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, disability, or veteran status.

We recognize that diversity strengthens our team, enriches our perspectives, and empowers us to better serve our clients and communities. At Finch Legal, inclusion isn’t just a value — it’s a practice.

source_jobs.title: Lead Paralegal
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FINCH

We believe every American household deserves access to counsel in life’s biggest moments. At Finch, we’re building the infrastructure to make justice radically more accessible. Our modern approach to consumer law automates the admin work and puts clients first, starting with personal injury.

In just over a year, we've grown 10x, raised a $20M Series A, and become the pre-litigation partner of choice for top personal injury firms across the country. We believe the best outcomes happen when expert operators and purpose-built AI work together – which is why we handle every step of pre-lit, from intake and claim opening to medical records, lien management, and demands, with humans leading every case.

We’re backed by Sequoia, Redpoint, and the founders & CEOs of generational companies like DoorDash, Ironclad, and Digits. We’re rebuilding how the law serves everyday Americans from first principles, and we’re hiring exceptional operators to help us scale it nationwide.




THIS ROLE

Lead Paralegals own attorney relationships, client communications, and the smooth progression of cases from sign-up to resolution. You’ll be the primary point of contact for both firms and clients, coordinating with the broader Legal Ops team to keep every case moving efficiently while ensuring the client experience is exceptional. You’ll provide regular updates, anticipate and remove bottlenecks, and ensure attorneys always have case information a their fingertips.




YOU WILL:

 - Serve as the primary liaison between Finch, attorneys, and clients

 - Maintain proactive communication with clients throughout treatment and the case lifecycle

 - Provide detailed case status reports and updates to attorneys and firms

 - Work with case managers, intake specialists, and demand reviewers to coordinate case progress

 - Identify and resolve delays or issues before they affect outcomes
   
   


YOUR TEAM: LEGAL OPERATIONS

Legal Operations is responsible for delivering exceptional outcomes for our law firm partners and their injured clients with precision, speed & care. At Finch, we’re building a team of the top 1% of legal operators with experience from law firms across America, and enabling them with next-generation technology to serve more clients, with more humanity.






YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU:

 - Have 10+ years of paralegal experience, ideally in personal injury or high-volume plaintiff work

 - Are bilingual in Spanish and English (preferred)

 - Are highly organized, detail-oriented, and proactive in communication

 - Can build trust quickly with attorneys and clients alike

 - Understand the importance of both client experience and case velocity

 - Are able to work Pacific Time (PST) hours to support client needs
   


COMPENSATION

$85,000 – $125,000 annual salary + equity




ADDITIONAL BENEFITS INCLUDE:

 - 100% coverage for health, dental, and vision

 - 401(k) retirement plan

 - In-office snacks, drinks, and daily team lunch and dinners

 - Flexible PTO (we trust you to take the time you need)
   
   


AT FINCH LEGAL, WE BELIEVE IN PRACTICING WHAT WE ADVOCATE.

As a company dedicated to upholding justice and protecting people in the workplace, we are equally committed to fostering a safe, inclusive, and equitable environment within our own walls. We welcome and support individuals from all backgrounds and lived experiences — regardless of race, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, disability, or veteran status.

We recognize that diversity strengthens our team, enriches our perspectives, and empowers us to better serve our clients and communities. At Finch Legal, inclusion isn’t just a value — it’s a practice.

source_jobs.title: Case Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FINCH

We believe every American household deserves access to counsel in life’s biggest moments. At Finch, we’re building the infrastructure to make justice radically more accessible. Our modern approach to consumer law automates the admin work and puts clients first, starting with personal injury.

In just over a year, we've grown 10x, raised a $20M Series A, and become the pre-litigation partner of choice for top personal injury firms across the country. We believe the best outcomes happen when expert operators and purpose-built AI work together – which is why we handle every step of pre-lit, from intake and claim opening to medical records, lien management, and demands, with humans leading every case.

We’re backed by Sequoia, Redpoint, and the founders & CEOs of generational companies like DoorDash, Ironclad, and Digits. We’re rebuilding how the law serves everyday Americans from first principles, and we’re hiring exceptional operators to help us scale it nationwide.


THIS ROLE

Case Managers ensure cases move forward quickly and comprehensively. You’ll focus on the operational heart of pre-litigation—opening claims, managing medical record requests, securing police reports, and resolving lien issues—so attorneys can work with complete, timely information.





WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Open insurance claims and manage communication with carriers

 - Request, track, and follow up on medical records and police reports

 - Monitor lien and subrogation issues, ensuring timely resolution

 - Partner with lead paralegals to prepare cases for demand drafting

 - Identify and fix operational bottleneck.




YOUR TEAM: LEGAL OPERATIONS

Legal Operations is responsible for delivering exceptional outcomes for our law firm partners and their injured clients with precision, speed & care. At Finch, we’re building a team of the top 1% of legal operators with experience from law firms across America, and enabling them with next-generation technology to serve more clients, with more humanity.





YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU:

 - Have 2+ years in legal operations, case management, or claims handling

 - Are process-driven and detail-oriented

 - Communicate effectively with both clients and attorneys

 - Thrive in fast-paced, high-volume environments

 - Are bilingual in Spanish/English (preferred, not required)




COMPENSATION

$60,000 – $85,000 annual salary + equity





ADDITIONAL BENEFITS INCLUDE:

 - 100% coverage for health, dental, and vision

 - 401(k) retirement plan

 - In-office snacks, drinks, and daily team lunch and dinners

 - Flexible PTO (we trust you to take the time you need)





AT FINCH LEGAL, WE BELIEVE IN PRACTICING WHAT WE ADVOCATE.

As a company dedicated to upholding justice and protecting people in the workplace, we are equally committed to fostering a safe, inclusive, and equitable environment within our own walls. We welcome and support individuals from all backgrounds and lived experiences — regardless of race, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, disability, or veteran status.

We recognize that diversity strengthens our team, enriches our perspectives, and empowers us to better serve our clients and communities. At Finch Legal, inclusion isn’t just a value — it’s a practice.

source_jobs.title: Strategy & Operations, General
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FINCH

We believe every American household deserves access to counsel in life’s biggest moments. At Finch, we’re building the infrastructure to make justice radically more accessible. Our modern approach to consumer law automates the admin work and puts clients first, starting with personal injury.

In just over a year, we've grown 10x, raised a $20M Series A, and become the pre-litigation partner of choice for top personal injury firms across the country. We believe the best outcomes happen when expert operators and purpose-built AI work together – which is why we handle every step of pre-lit, from intake and claim opening to medical records, lien management, and demands, with humans leading every case.

We’re backed by Sequoia, Redpoint, and the founders & CEOs of generational companies like DoorDash, Ironclad, and Digits. We’re rebuilding how the law serves everyday Americans from first principles, and we’re hiring exceptional operators to help us scale it nationwide.



THIS ROLE

 - Finch is building the operational engine that powers the best law firms in the country—and Strategy & Operations sits at the center of that mission. As part of this team, you'll work across multiple facets of our business to design the systems, workflows, and experiences that help firms run more efficiently, deliver better client outcomes, and scale with predictability.

 - This is a versatile role for someone who thrives at the intersection of process design, structured problem-solving, and cross-functional execution. You’ll dive deep into how work moves today, identify friction points, and craft high-leverage solutions that elevate the experience for firms, legal ops teams, and clients. Depending on your strengths and interests, your work may span case workflows, firm interfaces, legal ops experience, or broader operational scaling efforts.

 - You’ll blend analytical rigor with practical empathy—thinking holistically about the needs of attorneys, clients, and internal teams—while building systems that allow Finch to deliver fast, clear, high-quality outcomes as we grow.




YOU WILL:

 - Analyze existing workflows and operational patterns to identify bottlenecks, gaps, and opportunities for improvement across multiple parts of the business.

 - Design scalable processes, playbooks, and standards that create clarity, improve consistency, and support predictable case outcomes.

 - Define interfaces and communication models that make collaboration seamless—whether with firms assigning work, clients awaiting updates, or internal teams executing tasks.

 - Partner with product and engineering to translate operational needs into tools, automations, and app experiences that support high-quality execution.

 - Develop metrics and visibility mechanisms that help teams understand performance, uncover operational risks, and measure progress.

 - Collaborate across Case Workflow, Law Firm Experience, and Legal Ops Experience depending on company priorities and your strengths.

 - Test, iterate, and refine operational systems as volumes grow, firm needs evolve, and new product capabilities come online.

 - Contribute to Finch’s evolving operating model, helping us scale efficiently without sacrificing the human-touch experience firms and clients depend on.
   


YOUR TEAM: STRATEGY & OPERATIONS

S&O at Finch is the tip of the spear: we identify the most important problems and opportunities for growth, and solve them fast. We own playbooks, workflows, and experiments that move the business forward. You’ll work cross-functionally with Legal Ops, Product & Engineering, and Growth to deliver 10x improvements for our attorney partners, their clients, and our internal teams. We value velocity, grit, ownership, and — above all — optimism.



YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU:

 - Have 3-5+ years in a problem-solving role (e.g., consulting, BizOps, Strategy, Tech)

 - Have experience building zero-to-one systems or teams

 - Love navigating ambiguity and operatin

source_jobs.title: Machine Learning Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FINCH

We believe every American household deserves access to counsel in life’s biggest moments. At Finch, we’re building the infrastructure to make justice radically more accessible. Our modern approach to consumer law automates the admin work and puts clients first, starting with personal injury.

In just over a year, we've grown 10x, raised a $20M Series A, and become the pre-litigation partner of choice for top personal injury firms across the country. We believe the best outcomes happen when expert operators and purpose-built AI work together – which is why we handle every step of pre-lit, from intake and claim opening to medical records, lien management, and demands, with humans leading every case.

We’re backed by Sequoia, Redpoint, and the founders & CEOs of generational companies like DoorDash, Ironclad, and Digits. We’re rebuilding how the law serves everyday Americans from first principles, and we’re hiring exceptional operators to help us scale it nationwide.




THIS ROLE

Legal work is buried in unstructured documents, repetitive workflows, and data that no existing system handles well — and we're building the AI to fix it. As a Machine Learning Engineer at Finch, you'll own the full lifecycle of AI systems, from prototype to production, working on problems where a single breakthrough can meaningfully change how law firms operate.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

This is a hands-on applied AI role where you'll build and ship production systems — not just run experiments. The scope will grow as our product and team do.

 - Build voice agents, browser agents, OCR pipelines, and LLM-powered workflows that work reliably in production.

 - Design rigorous evaluation frameworks and feedback loops to systematically improve model accuracy and reliability.

 - Own the full ML lifecycle — model selection, fine-tuning, prompt design, deployment, and monitoring.

 - Collaborate directly with product, ops, and legal experts to make sure the AI is solving the right problems.

 - Track emerging research and tools, and make deliberate calls about when to bring them into our stack.




WHO WE HAVE IN MIND

This role is for engineers who care about outcomes over algorithms and are just as comfortable in production as they are in a notebook. Here's what that requires.


MUST-HAVES

 - 3+ years building and deploying production ML systems.

 - Strong Python skills and experience working across the ML stack end-to-end.

 - Hands-on experience with LLMs, prompt engineering, and evaluation design.

 - A track record of shipping observable, maintainable AI systems — not just prototypes.


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Experience with NLP, OCR, speech, or agent frameworks (LangChain, OpenAI APIs, etc.).

 - Prior work at an early-stage startup where you helped define ML infrastructure from scratch.

 - Familiarity with legal tech, document-heavy workflows, or regulated industries.


THIS ROLE MIGHT NOT BE FOR YOU IF

 - You prefer research or experimentation over owning systems in production.

 - You do your best work with a well-scoped problem and a stable, established ML platform.

 - You're looking for a remote-first role — this one is 4 days/week in our NYC office.




COMPENSATION

The expected package for this role includes base salary + commission + equity.

 - Base salary = $180,000 - $280,000.




BENEFITS

 - 100% coverage for health, dental, and vision.

 - 401(k) retirement plan.

 - In-office snacks, drinks, and daily team lunches and dinners.

 - Flexible PTO (we trust you to take the time you need).




INTERVIEW PROCESS

 1. Intro: An initial call to see if there's a mutual fit.

 2. Interview: An interview that typically involves a case assignment of sorts.

 3. Onsite: A visit to our NYC office to interview, meet the team, and have lunch.




AT FINCH LEGAL, WE BELIEVE IN PRACTICING WHAT WE ADVOCATE.

As a company dedicated to upholding justice and protecting people in the workplace, we are equally committed to fostering a safe, inclusive

source_jobs.title: Founding Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FINCH

We believe every American household deserves access to counsel in life’s biggest moments. At Finch, we’re building the infrastructure to make justice radically more accessible. Our modern approach to consumer law automates the admin work and puts clients first, starting with personal injury.

In just over a year, we've grown 10x, raised a $20M Series A, and become the pre-litigation partner of choice for top personal injury firms across the country. We believe the best outcomes happen when expert operators and purpose-built AI work together – which is why we handle every step of pre-lit, from intake and claim opening to medical records, lien management, and demands, with humans leading every case.

We’re backed by Sequoia, Redpoint, and the founders & CEOs of generational companies like DoorDash, Ironclad, and Digits. We’re rebuilding how the law serves everyday Americans from first principles, and we’re hiring exceptional operators to help us scale it nationwide.





THIS ROLE

Finch Legal's product touches people at their most vulnerable — injured clients navigating a system they don't understand, legal teams under pressure to move fast, and law firm partners who need to trust what they can't always see. Design is how we make all of that feel human. You'll be our first design hire, with a direct line to leadership and a real say in how the product takes shape.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

You'll own design across Finch's entire surface area — product, brand, and the emerging AI experiences in between. This role will grow as the company does; expect to define its scope as much as execute within it.

 - Build the internal case management tools that make our legal ops team faster and more capable

 - Design law firm-facing portals that give partners real visibility and control over their cases

 - Shape client experiences that make a complicated legal process feel clear and navigable

 - Define how Finch shows up publicly — brand, marketing site, and visual identity

 - Establish our earliest design system, creating a shared language of quality across product, design, and engineering




WHO WE HAVE IN MIND

This role demands someone who can set the design standard from scratch — not refine one that already exists. You'll need range, conviction, and the ability to move fast without sacrificing craft.


MUST-HAVES

 - 4+ years as a founding or sole designer, or 7+ years in a senior/lead design role with broad ownership

 - A portfolio that demonstrates strong product design, systems thinking, and visual craft — ideally across complex workflows

 - Proven ability to operate in 0→1 environments where the brief is rarely complete

 - Ability to work across product, brand, and visual design without a dedicated team around you


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Experience designing for operations-heavy or workflow-driven B2B products

 - Familiarity with AI surface design — particularly where trust, clarity, and error states matter

 - Prior experience building or contributing to a design system from the ground up


THIS ROLE MIGHT NOT BE FOR YOU IF

 - You do your best work when you're specializing — focused on one design discipline with dedicated peers around you

 - You prefer handing off polished specs and having others own implementation details

 - You're looking for a role where the brand, system, and design standards are already established




COMPENSATION

The expected package for this role includes base salary + equity.

 - Base salary = $160,000 - $225,000




BENEFITS

 - 100% coverage for health, dental, and vision.

 - 401(k) retirement plan.

 - In-office snacks, drinks, and daily team lunches and dinners.

 - Flexible PTO (we trust you to take the time you need).




INTERVIEW PROCESS

 1. Intro: An initial call to see if there's a mutual fit.

 2. Interview: An interview that typically involves a case assignment of sorts.

 3. Onsite: A visit to our NYC office to interview, meet the team, and have lunch.




AT FINCH LEGAL, W

source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FINCH

We believe every American household deserves access to counsel in life’s biggest moments. At Finch, we’re building the infrastructure to make justice radically more accessible. Our modern approach to consumer law automates the admin work and puts clients first, starting with personal injury.

In just over a year, we've grown 10x, raised a $20M Series A, and become the pre-litigation partner of choice for top personal injury firms across the country. THIS ROLE

Legal Assistants are flexible, plug-and-play operators who provide overflow support for Case Managers and paralegals across the legal ops team. Your day-to-day will vary — some days you're running treatment check-ins, other days you're covering a full desk while a teammate is out of office. If you thrive on variety and can context-switch quickly, this role is for you.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Solve problems across the case lifecycle wherever they arise

 - Conduct treatment check-ins with clients

 - Contact clients to get updates on case progression

 - Investigate coverage disputes, liability disputes, and coverage investigations

 - Step into a full desk when teammates are out of office

 - Handle high-volume overflow tasks across the legal ops team




YOUR TEAM: LEGAL OPERATIONS

Legal Operations is responsible for delivering exceptional outcomes for our law firm partners and their injured clients with precision, speed & care. At Finch, we’re building a team of the top 1% of legal operators with experience from law firms across America, and enabling them with next-generation technology to serve more clients, with more humanity.




YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU:

 - Have 1+ years in legal operations, case management, or claims handling in personal injury

 - Are bilingual (English/Spanish)

 - Are comfortable with a constantly changing day-to-day

 - Can manage high-volume workloads

 - Communicate effectively with both clients and attorneys

 - Thrive in fast-paced, high-volume environments




COMPENSATION

$50,000–$65,000 annual salary + equity




BENEFITS

 - 100% coverage for health, dental, and vision.

 - 401(k) retirement plan.

 - Flexible PTO (we trust you to take the time you need).

 - (For those in NYC) In-office snacks, drinks, and daily team lunch and dinners.




AT FINCH LEGAL, WE BELIEVE IN PRACTICING WHAT WE ADVOCATE.

We welcome and support individuals from all backgrounds and lived experiences — regardless of race, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, disability, or veteran status.

We recognize that diversity strengthens our team, enriches our perspectives, and empowers us to better serve our clients and communities. At Finch Legal, inclusion isn’t just a value — it’s a practice.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FINCH

We believe every American household deserves access to counsel in life’s biggest moments. At Finch, we’re building the infrastructure to make justice radically more accessible. Our modern approach to consumer law automates the admin work and puts clients first, starting with personal injury.

In just over a year, we've grown 10x, raised a $20M Series A, and become the pre-litigation partner of choice for top personal injury firms across the country. We believe the best outcomes happen when expert operators and purpose-built AI work together – which is why we handle every step of pre-lit, from intake and claim opening to medical records, lien management, and demands, with humans leading every case.

We’re backed by Sequoia, Redpoint, and the founders & CEOs of generational companies like DoorDash, Ironclad, and Digits. We’re rebuilding how the law serves everyday Americans from first principles, and we’re hiring exceptional operators to help us scale it nationwide.




THIS ROLE

Personal injury law is drowning in repetitive tasks, paper-based bottlenecks, and fragmented data – and we're building the software to fix it. As a Software Engineer at Finch, you'll own critical features end-to-end, ship fast, and work directly alongside product, ops, and design in a codebase where your decisions matter from day one.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

As a Software Engineer at Finch, you're building the system our team uses every day to run intake, medical records, police reports, lien management, and demands for personal injury firms across the country. Your code directly determines how many cases our paralegals can handle, and by extension, our ability to help more cases seen.

This is a full-stack engineering role at a company where the product is evolving quickly and engineers have real ownership – expect the scope to grow as we do.

 - Own major features end-to-end, from architecture decisions through production release.

 - Build reliable, performant systems across our core workflows using Python/Django, React, and AWS.

 - Ship AI-powered functionality that turns legal complexity into clear, usable software for law firms.

 - Collaborate directly with product, ops, and design to define what gets built and why.

 - Shape our engineering culture, standards, and practices while we're still small enough for it to matter.




WHO WE HAVE IN MIND

This role is for engineers who want ownership over outcomes, not just tickets. Here's what that requires.


MUST-HAVES

 - 3+ years building reliable, secure software in production.

 - Full-stack experience – comfortable across the frontend and backend.

 - Proficiency in Python and React, or demonstrably fast at picking up new stacks.

 - A track record of writing clean, testable, maintainable code.


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Experience with Django, AWS, or OpenAI APIs.

 - Prior work at an early-stage startup where you helped set engineering standards.

 - Familiarity with legal tech, document processing, or data-heavy workflows.


THIS ROLE MIGHT NOT BE FOR YOU IF

 - You do your best work with a well-defined spec and a stable, established codebase.

 - You prefer deep specialization in one layer of the stack over full-stack ownership.

 - You're looking for a remote-first role – this one is 4 days/week in our NYC office.




COMPENSATION

The expected package for this role includes base salary + equity.

 - Base salary = $180,000 - $280,000.




BENEFITS

 - 100% coverage for health, dental, and vision.

 - 401(k) retirement plan.

 - In-office snacks, drinks, and daily team lunches and dinners.

 - Flexible PTO (we trust you to take the time you need).




INTERVIEW PROCESS

 1. Intro: An initial call to see if there's a mutual fit.

 2. Interview: Technical session with an engineer on the team.

 3. Onsite: A visit to our NYC office to interview, meet the team, and have lunch.


AT FINCH LEGAL, WE BELIEVE IN PRACTICING WHAT WE ADVOCATE.

As a company dedicated to upholding justice and protect

In just over a year, we've grown 10x, raised a $20M Series A, and become the pre-litigation partner of choice for top personal injury firms across the country. We believe the best outcomes happen when expert operators and purpose-built AI work together – which is why we handle every step of pre-lit, from intake and claim opening to medical records, lien management, and demands, with humans leading every case.

We’re backed by Sequoia, Redpoint, and the founders & CEOs of generational companies like DoorDash, Ironclad, and Digits. We’re rebuilding how the law serves everyday Americans from first principles, and we’re hiring exceptional operators to help us scale it nationwide.




THIS ROLE

Finch has product-market fit and a pipeline that's growing faster than we can close it. We need an Account Executive who can own deals end-to-end — and help build the sales infrastructure around them. If you want your work to directly shape how a category gets defined, this is the role.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

This is a full-cycle sales role at a company where the playbook is still being written — you'll close deals and help build the system at the same time. The scope will grow as we do.

 - Own the full sales cycle from first conversation to signed contract.

 - Convert a fast-growing pipeline into long-term partnerships with law firm leaders.

 - Build trusted relationships with managing partners, firm owners, and ops leads who are rethinking how their practices run.

 - Shape Finch's GTM motion by feeding deal learnings directly into messaging, process, and product.

 - Represent Finch at legal tech events and conferences as the category grows.




WHO WE HAVE IN MIND

This role demands someone who closes with confidence and builds without a net. Here's what that looks like in practice.


MUST-HAVES

 - 4–7 years in a closing sales or BD role with a track record of exceeding quota.

 - Experience owning a full sales cycle end-to-end.

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills across multiple buyer types.


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Experience at a high-growth B2B SaaS startup.

 - Familiarity with the legal industry or selling into professional services firms.


THIS ROLE MIGHT NOT BE FOR YOU IF

 - You do your best work with an established playbook and mature sales infrastructure.

 - You'd rather execute within a defined system than help build one.




BENEFITS

 - 100% coverage for health, dental, and vision.

 - 401(k) retirement plan.

 - In-office snacks, drinks, and daily team lunches and dinners.

 - Flexible PTO (we trust you to take the time you need).




INTERVIEW PROCESS

 1. Intro: An initial call to see if there's a mutual fit.

 2. Interview: An interview that typically involves a case assignment of sorts.

 3. Onsite: A visit to our NYC office to interview, meet the team, and have lunch.




AT FINCH LEGAL, WE BELIEVE IN PRACTICING WHAT WE ADVOCATE.

As a company dedicated to upholding justice and protecting people in the workplace, we are equally committed to fostering a safe, inclusive, and equitable environment within our own walls. We welcome and support individuals from all backgrounds and lived experiences — regardless of race, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, disability, or veteran status.

We recognize that diversity strengthens our team, enriches our perspectives, and empowers us to better serve our clients and communities. At Finch Legal, inclusion isn’t just a value — it’s a practice.

source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FINCH

We believe every American household deserves access to counsel in life’s biggest moments. At Finch, we’re building the infrastructure to make justice radically more accessible. Our modern approach to consumer law automates the admin work and puts clients first, starting with personal injury.

In just over a year, we've grown 10x, raised a $20M Series A, and become the pre-litigation partner of choice for top personal injury firms across the country. THIS ROLE

Intake Specialists are the first voice potential clients hear from Finch. You’ll build trust in minutes, qualify cases, and ensure every eligible client signs quickly and seamlessly. This 24/7 team is the front line of Finch’s client experience.

 


YOU WILL:

 - Answer inbound calls with empathy and professionalism.

 - Qualify cases and confirm eligibility using defined criteria.

 - Guide clients through the sign-up process and secure retainers.

 - Hand off cases to attorneys and lead paralegals with complete and accurate information.

 - With your teammates, maintain responsiveness as a key builder of trust with every partner.

 


YOUR TEAM: LEGAL OPERATIONS

Legal Operations is responsible for delivering exceptional outcomes for our law firm partners and their injured clients with precision, speed & care. At Finch, we’re building a team of the top 1% of legal operators with experience from law firms across America, and enabling them with next-generation technology to serve more clients, with more humanity.

 


YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU:

 - Have 5+ years in client-facing intake or sales roles, ideally in legal services.

 - Are an excellent communicator who can build trust quickly.

 - Stay calm and focused under pressure.

 - Are comfortable working flexible schedules, including evenings and weekends.

 


COMPENSATION

The expected package for this role includes base salary + equity.

 - Base salary = $55,000 – $75,000.


AT FINCH LEGAL, WE BELIEVE IN PRACTICING WHAT WE ADVOCATE.

source_jobs.title: Case Manager, Liability, Evidence & Claims
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FINCH

We believe every American household deserves access to counsel in life’s biggest moments. At Finch, we’re building the infrastructure to make justice radically more accessible. Our modern approach to consumer law automates the admin work and puts clients first, starting with personal injury.

In just over a year, we've grown 10x, raised a $20M Series A, and become the pre-litigation partner of choice for top personal injury firms across the country. We believe the best outcomes happen when expert operators and purpose-built AI work together – which is why we handle every step of pre-lit, from intake and claim opening to medical records, lien management, and demands, with humans leading every case.

We’re backed by Sequoia, Redpoint, and the founders & CEOs of generational companies like DoorDash, Ironclad, and Digits. We’re rebuilding how the law serves everyday Americans from first principles, and we’re hiring exceptional operators to help us scale it nationwide.


THIS ROLE: CASE MANAGER, LIABILITY, EVIDENCE & CLAIMS

We are seeking an experienced Personal Injury Pre-Litigation Specialist to support case management from intake through settlement preparation. The ideal candidate has hands-on experience gathering and organizing liability evidence, conducting fault analysis, and managing ongoing communication with insurance carriers and government agencies.

This role also requires experience handling the full evidence and records collection process, including drafting and submitting FOIA and public records requests, obtaining police reports and supplemental narratives, securing dash cam/body cam footage and 911 audio, tracking outstanding records across jurisdictions, and ensuring accurate documentation throughout the lifecycle of the claim.

The ideal candidate is highly detail-oriented, organized, and energized by working in a fast-paced startup environment where processes are evolving and ownership is expected.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Open insurance claims with all relevant carriers (liability, UM/UIM, med pay), send letters of representation, obtain claim numbers and adjuster assignments, and manage ongoing carrier communication including acknowledgment follow-up

 - Draft and submit FOIA and public records requests to police departments, state agencies, and municipal offices; track request status, escalate non-responsive agencies, and ensure timely receipt of police reports, supplemental narratives, dash/body cam footage, and 911 recordings

 - Request and track medical records, witness statements, scene photographs, and other essential evidence; implement systematic follow-up protocols to ensure timely receipt and flag non-responsive parties for escalation

 - Conduct liability analysis from police reports, witness statements, and scene evidence; assess fault allocation, comparative negligence exposure, and evidentiary gaps; prepare comprehensive case packages for lead paralegals to draft settlement demands

 - Analyze workflow inefficiencies in evidence collection and case setup processes, identify automation opportunities, and implement process improvements to reduce cycle time from intake to demand-ready status


YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU:

 - Have experience in personal injury pre-litigation previously

 - Have experience drafting and managing FOIA / public records requests and obtaining police reports across multiple jurisdictions

 - Have experience opening claims, creating all coverage-related documentation, and managing relationships with insurance providers

 - Have experience conducting liability analysis and organizing evidence (police reports, photos, video, witness statements) into case-ready packages

 - Attention to detail and excitement for a fast-paced startup environment


COMPENSATION

 - $60,000 – $85,000 annual salary + equity




ADDITIONAL BENEFITS INCLUDE:

 - 100% coverage for health, dental, and vision

 - 401(k) retirement plan

 - In-office snacks, drinks, and daily team lunch and

source_jobs.title: Settlement Negotiator
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FINCH

We believe every American household deserves access to counsel in life’s biggest moments. At Finch, we’re building the infrastructure to make justice radically more accessible. Our modern approach to consumer law automates the admin work and puts clients first, starting with personal injury.

In just over a year, we've grown 10x, raised a $20M Series A, and become the pre-litigation partner of choice for top personal injury firms across the country. We believe the best outcomes happen when expert operators and purpose-built AI work together – which is why we handle every step of pre-lit, from intake and claim opening to medical records, lien management, and demands, with humans leading every case.

We’re backed by Sequoia, Redpoint, and the founders & CEOs of generational companies like DoorDash, Ironclad, and Digits. We’re rebuilding how the law serves everyday Americans from first principles, and we’re hiring exceptional operators to help us scale it nationwide.


THIS ROLE

Negotiators own the settlement phase of every case. You'll negotiate personal injury settlements with insurance carriers, advocate for clients during one of the most stressful times in their lives, and work to achieve fair, timely resolutions. Working closely with attorneys and case managers, you'll use strong negotiation skills, strategic thinking, and attention to detail to drive successful outcomes and help clients move forward with confidence.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Negotiate personal injury settlements by working collaboratively with insurance carriers and adjusters, pushing for the strongest outcome on every file

 - Build settlement breakdowns that reconcile gross recovery against fees, costs, and liens

 - Develop relationships with adjusters, clients, and attorneys while working with each stakeholder, always keeping the clients top of mind

 - Execute against agreed upon negotiation strategy with our attorney customers for each case
   


YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU:

 - Have 2+ years in PI settlement negotiation, BI adjusting, or paralegal work

 - Bring a maniacal sense of urgency and extreme ownership by chasing the answer rather than waiting for it

 - Are highly organized and can juggle dozens of competing deadlines and SLAs

 - Are comfortable with AI tools and concepts

 - Are excited about the opportunity to lay the foundation for this team’s operations
   


COMPENSATION

$60,000 – $75,000 annual salary + equity




ADDITIONAL BENEFITS INCLUDE:

 - 100% coverage for health, dental, and vision

 - 401(k) retirement plan

 - In-office snacks, drinks, and daily team lunch and dinners

 - Flexible PTO (we trust you to take the time you need)


AT FINCH LEGAL, WE BELIEVE IN PRACTICING WHAT WE ADVOCATE.

As a company dedicated to upholding justice and protecting people in the workplace, we are equally committed to fostering a safe, inclusive, and equitable environment within our own walls. We welcome and support individuals from all backgrounds and lived experiences — regardless of race, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, disability, or veteran status.

We recognize that diversity strengthens our team, enriches our perspectives, and empowers us to better serve our clients and communities. At Finch Legal, inclusion isn’t just a value — it’s a practice.

Hi 👋, I'm Molly https://www.linkedin.com/in/molly-hoffman-63a6b956/ – I run Marketing at Finch. I'm responsible for driving sales pipeline, and this role reports into me, with direct sales coaching from our founder, Viraj.

This role is open because our go-to-market org has real momentum – law firms are excited by what we're selling, and we need someone focused, disciplined, and hungry enough to turn that excitement into pipeline. Today our sales team is four people, with just one SDR.

As an early team member, you'll shape and execute the playbook for a major new business initiative, laying the groundwork for a repeatable SDR motion as we scale to 10, 20, 30 reps. You’ll also operate with autonomy and creativity, and have the opportunity to accelerate your career as a seller.




ABOUT FINCH

THIS ROLE

This is one of Finch's first SDR roles – which means you won't just be filling a seat, you'll be building the foundation for how we grow. You'll own top-of-funnel, help shape our outbound playbook, and be the first voice many law firms ever hear from us. If you want to do meaningful work early in your career and grow fast, this is the role.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

This is an outbound-focused role at a company where the GTM motion is still being defined –  you'll prospect and help build the system at the same time. Expect the scope to expand as we grow.

 - Own top-of-funnel pipeline through outbound email, cold calls, and LinkedIn outreach to law firm decision-makers.

 - Build Finch's SDR playbook – testing messaging, tracking performance, and iterating quickly.

 - Execute with discipline and consistency day to day – this role is as much about the daily grind as it is about strategy.

 - Convert early interest into booked meetings for our Account Executive team.

 - Maintain clean, accurate pipeline data in our CRM so the team can move fast with confidence.

 - Represent Finch at legal tech conferences and industry events.

source_jobs.title: Case Manager (IL/OH)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FINCH

We believe every American household deserves access to counsel in life’s biggest moments. At Finch, we’re building the infrastructure to make justice radically more accessible. Our modern approach to consumer law automates the admin work and puts clients first, starting with personal injury.

In just over a year, we've grown 10x, raised a $20M Series A, and become the pre-litigation partner of choice for top personal injury firms across the country. We believe the best outcomes happen when expert operators and purpose-built AI work together – which is why we handle every step of pre-lit, from intake and claim opening to medical records, lien management, and demands, with humans leading every case.

We’re backed by Sequoia, Redpoint, and the founders & CEOs of generational companies like DoorDash, Ironclad, and Digits. We’re rebuilding how the law serves everyday Americans from first principles, and we’re hiring exceptional operators to help us scale it nationwide.


THIS ROLE

Case Managers ensure cases move forward quickly and comprehensively. You’ll focus on the operational heart of pre-litigation—opening claims, managing medical record requests, securing police reports, and resolving lien issues—so attorneys can work with complete, timely information.
In this role, you'll primarily support personal injury cases in Illinois and Ohio, so experience working with personal injury cases in one or both of those states is essential.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Open insurance claims and manage communication with carriers

 - Request, track, and follow up on medical records and police reports

 - Monitor lien and subrogation issues, ensuring timely resolution

 - Partner with lead paralegals to prepare cases for demand drafting

 - Identify and fix operational bottleneck.




YOUR TEAM: LEGAL OPERATIONS

Legal Operations is responsible for delivering exceptional outcomes for our law firm partners and their injured clients with precision, speed & care. At Finch, we’re building a team of the top 1% of legal operators with experience from law firms across America, and enabling them with next-generation technology to serve more clients, with more humanity.




YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU:

 - Have 2+ years in legal operations, case management, or claims handling

 - Are process-driven and detail-oriented

 - Communicate effectively with both clients and attorneys

 - Thrive in fast-paced, high-volume environments

 - Are bilingual in Spanish/English (preferred, not required)

 - Have experience handling personal injury cases in Illinois or Ohio




COMPENSATION

$60,000 – $85,000 annual salary + equity




ADDITIONAL BENEFITS INCLUDE:

 - 100% coverage for health, dental, and vision

 - 401(k) retirement plan

 - In-office snacks, drinks, and daily team lunch and dinners

 - Flexible PTO (we trust you to take the time you need)





AT FINCH LEGAL, WE BELIEVE IN PRACTICING WHAT WE ADVOCATE.

As a company dedicated to upholding justice and protecting people in the workplace, we are equally committed to fostering a safe, inclusive, and equitable environment within our own walls. We welcome and support individuals from all backgrounds and lived experiences — regardless of race, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, disability, or veteran status.

We recognize that diversity strengthens our team, enriches our perspectives, and empowers us to better serve our clients and communities. At Finch Legal, inclusion isn’t just a value — it’s a practice.

notes: Finch is a legal tech startup with a focus on making justice more accessible. The company has a strong ops focus, with many case management and legal intake roles. There are some tech roles, but they are not the primary focus of the company.
